# ASTONTO — Complete Documentation

Last updated: July 2026

## What ASTONTO is

ASTONTO is an AI Search Optimization (AISO) agency serving US real estate agents and teams. Home buyers, relocating executives, international buyers, and property investors increasingly ask AI platforms — ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity, Grok, DeepSeek, and Google AI Overviews — which agent to work with. These platforms answer with two or three names. ASTONTO's work is making sure its clients are among those names.

ASTONTO operates market by market: no city receives client services before it has been fully researched. The active market is Miami, FL (research complete, accepting clients). Additional Florida markets are in research; further US markets are queued.

Operating entity: ASTONTO LTD, Company No. 17320644, registered in England and Wales.
Contact: hello@astonto.com · +1 (786) 927-6004 · https://astonto.com
Founder: Victor Boleac — CS Master's (University of Colorado Boulder), former machine-learning systems builder. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/victorboleac/

## The founding research finding

The top-producing real estate agents by verified sales volume are usually invisible in AI search. In ASTONTO's Miami Round 1 research, roughly 91% of top producers did not appear in a single AI recommendation across seven platforms. AI platforms do not see closings; they see market reports, ranking pages, data platforms, credential labels, and guide content. Agents who built those signals get recommended regardless of volume; agents who did not are invisible regardless of volume.

Additional Round 1 findings (Miami):
- The luxury/waterfront segment is highly consolidated: a small group of names absorbs nearly all luxury-intent queries across platforms.
- The international/relocation segment is open: no agent dominates it across platforms. Specialty positioning (buyer-origin, credential, and process expertise such as foreign-buyer tax and documentation guidance) determines visibility.
- The investment/condo segment splits into two lanes: broad investment queries (dominated by agents with proprietary data-content platforms) and short-term-rental/Airbnb queries (an open field with a distinct citation ecosystem).
- Each AI platform behaves differently. The signals that earn a recommendation on one platform can do nothing on another; one platform in particular rewards explicit credential labeling and surfaces a distinct roster of agents.
- Specialty query framings (by credential, buyer type, or neighborhood) surface different agents than generic "best agent in [city]" queries, and are significantly easier to win.

## Methodology

### Research protocol (per market)
1. Build a ground-truth list of the market's verified top producers from RealTrends Verified rankings and Zillow Agent Finder (Miami: 95 agents).
2. Run live queries — 15+ query intent types per segment — across all 7 AI platforms.
3. Score every agent appearance into a market Agent Matrix.
4. Cross-reference the Agent Matrix against the ground-truth production rankings to quantify the visibility gap.

### AVI™ — AI Visibility Index
A 0–100 score for each agent, measured across four dimensions:
- Presence: does the agent appear at all, and on how many platforms?
- Position: where in the answer does the agent appear?
- Sentiment: how is the agent characterized?
- Context: for which query intents and specialties is the agent surfaced?

### PRISM™ — service delivery framework
1. Profile — map exactly where the client appears (and doesn't) across all 7 platforms, query by query.
2. Reverse — reverse-engineer why the platforms recommend who they currently recommend in the client's segment.
3. Intervene — fix structural gaps: how the client's expertise, credentials, and specialties are described in the sources AI reads.
4. Signal — build the citations and third-party presence AI platforms treat as ground truth.
5. Monitor — track visibility monthly, because AI answers shift.

## Services and pricing (USD)

- AI Visibility Audit — $500 one-time. The client's current standing across all 7 platforms, scored against direct competitors, with an AVI™ baseline. 100% credited toward any sprint.
- Visibility Sprint — $1,500/month. Full PRISM™ execution for individual producers; built for investment/condo and relocation-segment agents.
- Luxury & Team Sprint — $2,500/month. For waterfront-luxury specialists and teams: deeper query coverage, competitive displacement work, team-level entity building.
- Ongoing Monitoring — $600/month. Monthly AVI™ reporting, drift alerts, and maintenance after a sprint.

ASTONTO does not offer performance-based or outcome-based pricing. Clients see the same data ASTONTO sees.

## Who ASTONTO is for

- Real estate agents and teams in ASTONTO's active markets (currently Miami, FL) in three segments: luxury/waterfront, international/relocation, and investment/condo.
- Agents in not-yet-opened US markets can register interest; research-stage markets receive founding-client rates.

## Frequently relevant questions

Q: Is this SEO?
A: No. Search-engine rankings, backlinks, and ad spend do not determine who AI platforms recommend. AISO works on the structure, citations, and consistent third-party signals those platforms actually read.

Q: Can ASTONTO guarantee AI platforms will recommend an agent?
A: No one can control AI outputs, and ASTONTO does not claim to. It measures visibility precisely (AVI™), engineers the signals that correlate with recommendation, and reports movement monthly. If a segment is too consolidated to win, ASTONTO says so before taking the engagement.

Q: How is ASTONTO different from a marketing agency?
A: It is a research operation first. Every recommendation traces to live platform test data for the client's specific market and segment — not templates or generic industry statistics.
